commit.message:
fix: detect CMS URL changes and reconnect automatically (#87) (#89)

Add a config watch coroutine that polls cms_config.json every 5 seconds.
When the CMS URL changes (e.g. via the Settings page), the client now
closes the WebSocket gracefully and reconnects to the new URL — no
service restart needed.

- Add _config_watch_loop() that runs alongside the message loop
- Clear auth token on URL change (new CMS won't recognise it)
- Remove fragile sudo systemctl restart from the API endpoint
- Add unit tests for config watch detection
- Add integration tests with real WebSocket servers
- Add Playwright E2E tests for Settings page config save
- Bump version to 1.4.1

Fixes #87

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add --speed flag for replay speed control

New CLI option: --speed <multiplier>
  0 = max speed (default for CLI)
  1 = real-time (original PCAP timing)
  5 = 5x accelerated (default for Docker Compose)

Docker Compose defaults to 5x — fast enough to reach RealTime state
quickly while slow enough for interactive WebSocket testing.

Override: REPLAY_SPEED=1 docker compose up

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Bundle non-guaranteed shared libraries in release

Copy libcrypt.so.2, libreadline.so.8, libffi.so.8, libtcl8.6.so, and
libtinfo.so.6 from the manylinux_2_34 build container into lib/ and
patch affected ELFs with $ORIGIN-relative RUNPATHs so the dynamic
linker finds the bundled copies on target systems that don't have these
libraries installed by default (e.g. Ubuntu lacks libcrypt.so.2).

Also adds patchelf to the dnf install list.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add deterministic pre-activation issue-slot gating for schema-coverage-rotation (#2210)

* feat: add deterministic pre-activation issue-slot gating for schema-coverage-rotation

Move schema-coverage issue capacity calculation into a deterministic pre-activation
step, skipping the agent job entirely when no issue slots remain. Extracts the
issue-slot logic into a unit-testable helper module under .github/workflows-src/lib/,
adds a thin inline wrapper script, and authors the workflow source under
.github/workflows-src/schema-coverage-rotation/ following the openspec-verify-label
templated pattern. Publishes open_schema_coverage_issues, issue_slots_available, and
gate_reason as pre-activation outputs consumed by job conditions and the agent prompt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(openspec): archive schema-coverage-issue-slot-gating via verify-openspec

commit.message:
Fix refresh line alignment: use linear wall-clock to chart-time mapping

Instead of assuming CDT timezone, build a linear mapping from the load
test's own Timestamp/StartTimeMs columns. This works regardless of what
timezone the QueryRunner writes timestamps in. Strip timezone from
refresh UTC timestamps for naive comparison.

commit.message:
Add frontend viewer and Docker Compose setup

Frontend (frontend/index.html):
- Single-file SPA with binary WebSocket protocol support
- Subscribe/Get/Unsubscribe with DataFlags (Book, Info, or both)
- Real-time order book display with bid/ask price levels
- InfoSnapshot display with all 22 fields
- Event log (last 500 entries) with color-coded message types
- Configurable price decimals for mantissa formatting
- Dark theme, no external dependencies

Docker Compose:
- backend: .NET multi-stage build, replays PCAP with --ws-port
- frontend: nginx:alpine serving static HTML
- Configurable via env vars: PCAP_PREFIX, WS_PORT, FRONTEND_PORT
- PCAP files mounted as volume (not in image)

Usage:
  docker compose up --build
  Open http://localhost:3000, connect to ws://localhost:8080/ws

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: include db/ directory in publish output for schema sync

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Write-Output corrupting scan JSON stdout

Write-Output sends to stream 1 (stdout), which gets redirected into
scan.json.tmp, prepending '::warning::...' before the JSON and making
it unparseable. Change to Write-Host which goes to the console/info
stream and doesn't corrupt the redirected output.

This caused all repos to fail validation on the first run after PR #55
because they all had old scan.json without _cache_version, triggering
the incremental-disabled warning path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Grey out event select when inherit is selected

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add coding standards to copilot-instructions based on review feedback analysis

Analyzed Copilot code review feedback across all 44 merged PRs and identified
recurring themes. Added targeted coding standards to reduce future review
findings:

- HTML/XSS safety (flagged in 14 PRs): escaping rules, innerHTML vs textContent
- Accessibility (8 PRs): aria-labels, contrast, keyboard navigation
- GitHub API pagination (4 PRs): always paginate, rate limit caching
- JavaScript patterns (4 PRs): URL encoding, parseInt NaN, regex caching
- PowerShell patterns (5 PRs): Select-Object -Unique, += in loops, -match case
- Test quality (4 PRs): no fixed sleeps, isolate state, semantic assertions
- Duplicated logic (5 PRs): extract shared helpers
- Documentation accuracy (8 PRs): keep comments/docs in sync with code

Consolidated existing PowerShell Gotchas into the new PowerShell Patterns section.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Event dropdowns: separate inherit vs UNSET options

- '✕ UNSET (disable)' = writes UNSET to metadata, explicitly disables inherited value

commit.message:
feat: fin_tax_document_accounts join table for multi-account PDF linking (#194)

* feat: fin_tax_document_accounts join table for multi-account PDF linking (#186)

Implements the `fin_tax_document_accounts` join table as the canonical
source for document–account associations, replacing the legacy
`fin_tax_documents.account_id` column. Adds a multi-account consolidated
PDF import flow backed by a new `tax_form_multi_account_import` GenAI job
type, with server-side account matching and a three-phase upload/poll/assign
UI modal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback - route ordering, indexes, null checks, S3 defer, AI normalization

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bherila/2025-website/sessions/8a2a506a-5e42-4acf-9c40-9237e4c7b289

Co-authored-by: bherila <1422004+bherila@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: multi-account consolidated 1099 import with 1099-B lot extraction

- Render one button per account_link (not per document) in TaxDocuments1099Section,
  so consolidated broker PDFs with multiple form types show individual entries
- Add "Consolidated 1099 (Broker)" upload option per account, routing through
  MultiAccountImportModal with preselectedAccountId for single-account mode
- Enhance AI prompt to extract individual 1099-B transaction lots from broker PDFs
- Add upsertLotsFromBroker() in ParseImportJob: idempotent lot + sell line item
  creation keyed on tax_document_id for re-import safety
- Add tax_document_id FK to fin_account_lots for traceability
- Add BrokerTransaction1099B / Broker1099BParsedData types to tax-document.ts
- Fix SQLite schema dump to include file_hash on files_for_fin_accounts
- Update AGENTS.md: always pass --database=sqlite to migrate and schema:dump

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: IncidentIQ is an OpenEnv RL environment where AI agents act as SREs to diagnose and fix production incidents. It tests reasoning under uncertainty, red-herring resistance, and precise remediation using deterministic, reproducible scoring.

commit.message:
feat: prefetch radio tracks on second-to-last item in queue

Instead of fetching tracks only when the last track finishes (adding
~740ms delay), start prefetching when we begin playing the second-to-last
track. The prefetched data is used instantly on track completion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: expand blocker severity to cover codebase health issues (#65)

The review bot classified a committed generated artifact
(.copilot-session.md) as a 💡 Suggestion when it should have been a 🚫
Blocker. Root cause: the severity definition only listed 'bugs,
security, breaking changes' — anything outside that list got downgraded.

Expanded blocker criteria to include codebase health: generated files
committed to repo, test pollution, dead code that misleads, wrong
abstractions that will spread.

fix(pipeline): address Phase 5 review findings (#390)

* fix(pipeline): address Phase 5 review findings

IV-001: DiscussionPipeline.handle_error signature BaseException → Exception
        to match Pipeline protocol contract
IV-003: ReviewPipeline sync logging → async (ainfo, awarning, aerror)
        consistent with Discussion/CodingPipeline
IV-006: Extract format_diff_text() helper — eliminates duplicated diff
        formatting in ReviewPipeline.prepare
IV-009: Route CodingPipeline error messages through user_error_message()
        to prevent token/detail leakage in Jira comments

890 tests pass, 93.63% coverage, lint clean, pyright clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(pipeline): consolidate error handling, remove python-gitlab entirely

Review findings (IV-001 through IV-012):
- IV-001: DiscussionPipeline.handle_error BaseException → Exception
- IV-002: Replace assert with stage_requires() type-narrowing helper
- IV-003: ReviewPipeline sync logging → async
- IV-004: Standardize cleanup to shutil.rmtree across all pipelines
- IV-006: Extract format_diff_text() helper
- IV-007: Replace mr_iid or 0 with proper type narrowing
- IV-008: Remove python-gitlab from all source, tests, and scripts
- IV-009: Route CodingPipeline errors through user_error_message()

Consolidation:
- Extract post_pipeline_error() shared helper — eliminates duplicated
  error handling across 3 pipelines
- stage_requires[T]() returns narrowed type for pyright strict mode
- Rewrite comment_poster.py from python-gitlab sync to GitLabClient async
- Rewrite aca_integration.py E2E with GitLabTestClient httpx wrapper
- Rewrite gitlab_provisioner.py and demo scripts with httpx.Client
- Remove python-gitlab from pyproject.toml dependencies

891 tests pass, 93.63% coverage, lint clean, pyright clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
patch: v6.0.2 — Copilot review fixes + reauth token rediscovery (#135)

* fix(hooks): BSD grep compat + docs corrections for loop guard

- Replace \s* with [[:space:]]* in telegram-loop-guard.sh for macOS/BSD grep
- Fix settings.json → settings.local.json in hook comment
- Clarify VS Code hooks ship with repo (no copy needed for cloners)
- Fix troubleshooting section to be host-specific

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(actions): bump pnpm/action-setup from 5.0.0 to 6.0.0

Bumps [pnpm/action-setup](https://github.com/pnpm/action-setup) from 5.0.0 to 6.0.0.
- [Release notes](https://github.com/pnpm/action-setup/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/pnpm/action-setup/compare/fc06bc1257f339d1d5d8b3a19a8cae5388b55320...08c4be7e2e672a47d11bd04269e27e5f3e8529cb)

---
updated-dependencies:
- dependency-name: pnpm/action-setup
  dependency-version: 6.0.0
  dependency-type: direct:production
  update-type: version-update:semver-major
...

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>

* chore(actions): bump docker/build-push-action from 7.0.0 to 7.1.0

Bumps [docker/build-push-action](https://github.com/docker/build-push-action) from 7.0.0 to 7.1.0.
- [Release notes](https://github.com/docker/build-push-action/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/docker/build-push-action/compare/d08e5c354a6adb9ed34480a06d141179aa583294...bcafcacb16a39f128d818304e6c9c0c18556b85f)

---
updated-dependencies:
- dependency-name: docker/build-push-action
  dependency-version: 7.1.0
  dependency-type: direct:production
  update-type: version-update:semver-minor
...

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>

* fix: replace prescriptive session/start hints with help(topic: 'startup') chain

* fix(ci): pin pnpm v10 for action-setup v6 compatibility

pnpm/action-setup v6.0.0 may default to pnpm v11 which uses a different
lockfile format. Pin version: 10 to match packageManager field and
lockfile format v9.0. Also fix stale version comment (v4 → v6).

* fix: strip markdown fluff from help topics, add token save hint

Help topics now use plain text headings instead of markdown headers.
Removed unnecessary bold, backticks, and formatting in topic content.
Newlines preserved for readability, tables and code blocks kept.

session_start hint now says 'Save this token.' before the help reference.
Tests updated to match.

* feat(session): auto-dismiss reauth dialog on valid token (30-475)

When a tool call succeeds after AUTH_FAILED, the reauth dialog is now
automatically deleted. Stores msgId at dialog creation, clears it on
approval/denial, and auth hook composite-touches session + dismisses
any pending dialog on success.

2200/2200 tests passing. Minor: vi.fn<> TS type errors in
reauth-dismiss.test.ts deferred (runtime passing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(quality): TMCP message response standard + quality sweep (10-474)

Creates docs/message-response-standard.md — authoritative reference for
all user-facing text. Audits and fixes 27 src/ files: error messages,
hints, tool descriptions, TOOL_INDEX entries.

Key changes:
- Removed behavioral directives from 5 tool descriptions
- Removed retired edit_message_text from TOOL_INDEX + docs/design.md
- AUTH_FAILED message now actionable per standard
- Fixed stale JSDoc in animation-state.ts

2195/2195 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): correct vi.fn generic syntax in reauth-dismiss.test.ts (40-478)

Replace two-arg tuple syntax vi.fn<[Args], Return>() with single
function type vi.fn<(arg) => Return>() for Vitest 2.x compatibility.
tsc --strict passes; 2200/2200 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(tasks): commit untracked backlog, completed tasks, and draft doc

6 backlog tasks, 1 completed task (10-472), release announcement draft.
Operator-authorized — previously untracked in dev.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(v6.0.2): Copilot review fixes + reauth token rediscovery hints (10-479)

- help.ts: startup guidance — 'session-bound calls' not 'all calls', message/history needs count:20
- help.test.ts: added startup topic test coverage
- session_start.test.ts: updated stale 'instructions field' comment to 'hint field'
- reauth-dismiss.test.ts: afterEach resets auth hook to prevent cross-test leak
- session_start.ts: reauth deny encourages token rediscovery from memory
- session_start.ts: reconnect dialog hints at saved token
- ci.yml: removed version:10, action reads packageManager field
- changelog: v6.0.1 + v6.0.2 entries
- package.json: bumped to 6.0.2

2201/2201 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(errors): replace legacy tool names with v6 action() paths in error strings

Addresses 18 Copilot review findings on PR #135. Error/recovery strings
across 12 source files referenced removed MCP tools (session_start,
list_sessions, save_profile, list_reminders). Replaced with v6 action()
dispatcher equivalents throughout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): downgrade pnpm/action-setup to v4 with pinned version

pnpm/action-setup@v6 was incompatible — caused ERR_PNPM_BROKEN_LOCKFILE
on CI. Downgraded to v4 (fe02b34f) with explicit version: '10.0.0'
for reproducibility.

* fix(text): 4 minor text corrections from PR #135 Copilot review

session-gate SID_REQUIRED spacing, NAME_CONFLICT missing name param,
cancel_reminder id field references action path, test count 2195→2201.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(docs): correct CI changelog entry — v4 downgrade + version pin

Changelog incorrectly stated version pin was removed. Actual: pnpm/action-setup
downgraded v6→v4 with explicit version: '10.0.0'.

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: OpenAgents
repository.description: OpenAgents is a docker-first orchestration platform for running agent workflows from a simple web UI.

commit.message:
feat(api): use RenderedCompactJsonFormatter for structured logging

Switch from JsonFormatter to RenderedCompactJsonFormatter which includes
the rendered message (@m) by default, uses compact keys (@t, @l, @mt),
and produces ~30-40% smaller log payloads. Properties are flattened to
root level instead of nested under Properties object.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add theme system with light/dark/seasonal modes

- Create ThemeContext with 5 theme options: light, dark, summer, fall, spring
- Add theme selector to settings menu with visual color swatches
- Implement theme persistence via localStorage
- Update App.tsx to wrap with ThemeProvider
- Refactor Root.tsx to use useTheme hook instead of hardcoded dark mode
- Expand settings panel with separate Home Team and Theme sections

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
